purpose: Vite entry point. Mounts <AppProvider><App/></AppProvider> into #root and installs the Electron fetch shim that intercepts /api/* requests and routes them through window.electronAPI IPC handlers. Imports global stylesheets (index.css) and the four IDE-specific stylesheets eagerly so they're available before IDE view mounts. The fetch shim is the bridge between Synapse's standalone-server mental model (where components fetch /api/*) and the Electron-app reality (where data flows over IPC).
gotchas: Fetch shim runs ONLY if window.electronAPI exists — components built for browser-served standalone mode would skip the shim and hit the real /api/* endpoints (only useful if dev server proxies to src/server).
Shim's routeToIPC returns null for unmapped /api/* URLs and falls through to native fetch — silent failure if you forget to add a route, you'll see 404s instead of an obvious error.
Shim ALWAYS returns ok:true status:200 — error responses from IPC are NOT translated into HTTP-style failures; consumers checking response.ok will be misled.
Body parameter in shim is unused — POST endpoints like /api/dashboards/{id}/clear ignore the body, relying on the IPC method to know what to do with just the id.

IDE stylesheets are imported globally so the bundle always pays for them even if the user never opens IDE — moving these to lazy imports requires lazy-loading IDEView and the four CSS modules together.
AppProvider must be the outermost wrapper for both AppContext and DispatchContext — they live in the same provider component.
patterns: Fetch interception via window.fetch reassignment guarded by window.electronAPI presence — same pattern works for any Electron-React app needing browser-style fetch over IPC.
URL pattern matching via regex (e.g. /^\/api\/dashboards\/([^/]+)\/(.+)$/) — extend by adding a new regex match in routeToIPC.
createRoot + .render with single nested provider — no React.StrictMode wrapper (intentional given IPC listener side-effects).
conventions: CSS imports happen alongside the entry, not inside components.
IPC route helpers placed inside main.jsx scope (not exported) — keep this file as the only fetch shim location.
